project.name: komga-reader
project.description: Native Android e-ink reader app (Kotlin/Jetpack Compose) for Onyx Boox devices, unifying paged-comic, webtoon-scroll and EPUB-reflow reading with native Komga and OPDS sources plus Onyx e-ink optimization, behind a source- and device-agnostic multi-module architecture built on two seams (MediaSource and Document/Viewer/EinkController).

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build script for the top-level app module, wiring Compose, Hilt, navigation, Room and the multi-module dependencies (domain, sources, render-core, eink-onyx, data).
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin DSL Gradle script; the app module is the imperative shell that wires all other modules together.

nodes.name: DownloadInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented E2E test exercising the full download-then-offline-render path: fetches a book from a live Komga server, stores it via DownloadManager, reads it back and renders the first page with MuPDF.
nodes.languageNotes: AndroidJUnit4 instrumented test hitting a real local test-Komga at 10.0.2.2:25600 (emulator host loopback).

nodes.name: EpubReaderInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented E2E test downloading an EPUB from the live test-Komga and verifying MuPDF renders a non-empty first page.

nodes.name: LibraryFlowInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented E2E test for the library-browsing flow against a live Komga server, persisting server config and listing series.

nodes.name: ReaderFlowInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented E2E test for the reader flow, validating read-progress handling against a live Komga server.

nodes.name: SetupServerTest.kt
nodes.summary: One-time setup test that writes the real NAS server configuration into the app Room database using the same DB name, migrations and keystore alias as production DataModule.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the production DB name and migration list rather than an in-memory DB so the persisted config survives for the app.

nodes.name: AndroidManifest.xml
nodes.summary: Android application manifest declaring the Hilt Application class, MainActivity entry point, permissions and app-level configuration.

nodes.name: ColorPipelineTransformation.kt
nodes.summary: Coil image Transformation that applies the full E-Ink pixel pipeline (saturation, contrast, gamma, sharpen, dither) to decoded reader-page bitmaps, with a profile-derived cache key for per-image+profile caching.

nodes.name: AuthHeaders.kt
nodes.summary: Builds HTTP auth headers for Coil cover/page requests from a ServerConfig, preferring API key and falling back to HTTP Basic.

nodes.name: KomgaSourceProvider.kt
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a concrete KomgaSource from a persisted ServerConfig, returning null when not connected. Bridges the app shell to Seam A (sources).

nodes.name: LocalLibrary.kt
nodes.summary: Extension functions that assemble an offline series list from downloaded books, grouping by series and exposing the set of series IDs that have local downloads for the offline badge.
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin extension functions on List<DownloadedBook> — pure transformations, no side effects.

nodes.name: AppModule.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt DI module providing the Coil ImageLoader and the device-gated EinkController — OnyxEinkController on Boox hardware, NoOpEinkController otherwise (Seam B selection).
nodes.languageNotes: Hardware-gates the EinkController via Build.MANUFACTURER so development never crashes on non-Boox devices.

nodes.name: HardwareButtons.kt
nodes.summary: Singleton HardwareButtonBus that distributes physical button events (volume keys = page turn) to the active reader via a MutableSharedFlow.

nodes.name: NoOpEinkController.kt
nodes.summary: Fallback EinkController for non-Boox devices and the emulator: reports no e-ink capability and no-ops refresh/contrast/invert while still relaying hardware button events from the bus.
nodes.languageNotes: Null-Object pattern implementation of the domain EinkController interface (Seam B).

nodes.name: Localization.kt
nodes.summary: Tiny i18n helper exposing the LocalStrings composition local / language plumbing for the app.

nodes.name: Strings.kt
nodes.summary: Central type-safe i18n string table for the app with German and English variants, the Language enum and stringsFor() lookup, enforcing compile-time DE/EN parity.
nodes.languageNotes: Type-safe localization via a Kotlin data class of string keys with DE+EN instances; compile-time parity instead of Android string resources.

nodes.name: KomgaReaderApp.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt application entry point that initializes DI and logs whether the device is Onyx hardware (active e-ink mode) or a no-op fallback.

nodes.name: MainActivity.kt
nodes.summary: Single-activity entry point hosting the Compose navigation graph (home, series, group, reader routes), wiring hardware volume keys to page turns, fullscreen immersive mode, theme, language, e-ink mode and the active color filter.
nodes.languageNotes: Provides app-wide CompositionLocals (strings, e-ink mode, image filter, color profile) and overrides onKeyDown to convert volume keys into page-turn ButtonEvents.

nodes.name: AnchoredMenuPopup.kt
nodes.summary: Flat E-Ink popup container anchored to an absolute window offset with a custom PopupPositionProvider that clamps inside the window and supports start/end edge alignment.

nodes.name: EinkBottomBar.kt
nodes.summary: Onyx-look floating bottom navigation bar with equal-width items (icon over label), a black accent bar on the active item, flat with no Material indicator pill and no animation.

nodes.name: EinkButtons.kt
nodes.summary: EinkOutlinedButton wrapper giving every outlined button a consistent 1.5dp hairline border for legibility on e-ink, replacing the too-thin Material default.

nodes.name: EinkComponents.kt
nodes.summary: Reusable Onyx-look E-Ink building blocks: settings tiles, section headers, checkmark choice rows, ± stepper rows and a sub-page scaffold — all flat, hairline-bordered, animation-free.

nodes.name: EinkModal.kt
nodes.summary: Onyx-look modal dialogs replacing the bare Material AlertDialog: EinkModal with confirm/dismiss actions and EinkInfoDialog for read-only info, both with a strong black border and single-modal discipline.

nodes.name: EinkSearchBar.kt
nodes.summary: Slim E-Ink search field: a low rounded pill with hairline border built on BasicTextField, placeholder, optional clear button and a search icon, submitting on IME-Search.

nodes.name: FilteredImage.kt
nodes.summary: App-wide color-filter image wrappers: FilteredAsyncImage (Coil) and FilteredImage (MuPDF bitmap) apply the active ColorProfile's filter, plus the LocalImageFilter composition local and a ColorProfile-to-ColorFilter converter.

nodes.name: onCreate
nodes.summary: Sets up immersive fullscreen and the Compose content: provides app-wide CompositionLocals and builds the NavHost with home, series, group and reader routes.

nodes.name: onKeyDown
nodes.summary: Translates physical volume-up/down key presses into PAGE_PREV/PAGE_NEXT button events on the HardwareButtonBus.

nodes.name: HardwareButtonBus
nodes.summary: Injectable singleton broadcasting hardware button events to the active reader through a buffered MutableSharedFlow.

nodes.name: NoOpEinkController
nodes.summary: No-op EinkController implementation for non-Boox devices, reporting no e-ink hardware and relaying button events from the bus.

nodes.name: ColorPipelineTransformation
nodes.summary: Coil Transformation applying the E-Ink pixel pipeline to a decoded bitmap with a profile-derived cache key, used only on reader-page requests.

nodes.name: SettingsTile
nodes.summary: Hairline-bordered settings tile composable with leading icon, title, summary and optional drill-in chevron.

nodes.name: ChoiceRow
nodes.summary: Selectable row composable using a trailing checkmark instead of a radio circle, with query highlighting for search matches.

nodes.name: SubPageScaffold
nodes.summary: Scaffold composable for settings sub-pages with a back-arrow top bar and a scrolling padded body.

nodes.name: EinkBottomBar
nodes.summary: Floating Onyx-look bottom navigation bar rendering equal-width nav cells with an active accent bar.

nodes.name: EinkModal
nodes.summary: Onyx-look modal dialog with title, content slot and split confirm/dismiss action buttons replacing Material AlertDialog.

nodes.name: EinkInfoDialog
nodes.summary: Read-only Onyx-look info dialog with a title and a single close X, for displaying values without an action.

nodes.name: EinkSearchBar
nodes.summary: Slim E-Ink search field built on BasicTextField with hairline pill styling, optional clear button and IME-search submission.

nodes.name: AnchoredMenuPopup
nodes.summary: Bordered flat E-Ink popup container positioned at an absolute window anchor.

nodes.name: EinkOutlinedButton
nodes.summary: Outlined button wrapper applying a consistent 1.5dp hairline border for e-ink legibility.

nodes.name: FilteredAsyncImage

nodes.name: localSeries

nodes.name: einkController
nodes.summary: Hilt provider selecting OnyxEinkController on Boox hardware or NoOpEinkController otherwise, wiring the refresher to the concrete controller.

nodes.name: FilteredReaderImage.kt
nodes.summary: Reader image composable that applies the e-ink color pipeline (saturation/contrast/brightness) to decoded page bitmaps before display.
nodes.languageNotes: Jetpack Compose composable wiring the domain PixelPipeline / ColorProfile into image rendering.

nodes.name: HighlightText.kt
nodes.summary: Small text composable that highlights matching substrings, used by the settings search to mark query hits.

nodes.name: LoadingIndicator.kt
nodes.summary: E-ink-aware loading indicator that shows a static text in e-ink mode and a spinner otherwise; hosts the LocalEinkMode composition local.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines LocalEinkMode CompositionLocal — single source of truth for animation gating across the UI.

nodes.name: StatusCluster.kt
nodes.summary: Composable rendering a cluster of status badges/labels for a series or library entry.

nodes.name: TypeFilterMenu.kt

nodes.summary: Navigation route and screen composable for browsing the series of a single library group, wiring GroupBrowseViewModel state to a series grid.

nodes.summary: ViewModel that loads the series of one library group from the active MediaSource, falling back to locally downloaded works when the server is offline.

nodes.name: GroupsScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Screen listing user-defined library groups (shelves) with cover collages and an e-ink modal for creating/editing groups.

nodes.name: GroupsViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: ViewModel managing the list of library groups (shelves): loads cover collages and container lists, and creates/updates/deletes groups.

nodes.name: HomeScreen.kt

nodes.name: AppIcons.kt
nodes.summary: Central semantic icon registry (single source of truth) mapping purpose-named icons to concrete Lucide glyphs so glyphs can be swapped without touching call sites.
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin object acting as SSOT; semantic names decouple call sites from the underlying Lucide glyph.

nodes.name: LucideIcons.kt

nodes.summary: Screen rendering the full series library as a grid with download badges and offline state, driven by LibraryViewModel.

nodes.summary: ViewModel for the main library: browses series via the active MediaSource, resolves effective content types, falls back to local works offline, and drives series downloads.

nodes.name: SeriesFilter.kt
nodes.summary: Pure helper filtering a series list by content type and search query for the library view.

nodes.name: PluginsScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Placeholder screen for the future runtime plugin/source loader (Phase 4).

nodes.name: ComicPageLoader.kt
nodes.summary: Decodes a comic page via Coil, downscales it, and runs the PanelDetector to produce reading-ordered panel rectangles for guided view.

nodes.name: ComicReaderScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Guided comic reader screen rendering full pages and zoomed panels with tap zones, e-ink refresh, and optional panel overlay.

nodes.name: ComicReaderViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: ViewModel driving the guided comic reader: detects and caches per-page panels, handles tap/hardware navigation between panels and pages, and manages zoom and chrome state.

nodes.name: EinkReaderEffect.kt
nodes.summary: Compose side-effect that wires reader lifecycle to the Onyx e-ink refresher (mode setup and teardown).

nodes.name: EpubReaderScreen.kt
nodes.summary: EPUB/reflow reader screen rendering rendered pages with the e-ink color filter and loading indicator.

nodes.name: PagedReaderScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Paged comic reader screen presenting one filtered page at a time with tap zones and Onyx e-ink refresh on page turn.

nodes.name: ReaderChrome.kt
nodes.summary: Shared reader chrome composable (top/bottom bars and controls) toggled by center tap across the reader variants.

nodes.name: ReaderContent.kt
nodes.summary: Thin model/holder describing the content a reader displays (e.g. webtoon strip and source) passed into the reader composables.

nodes.name: ReaderEinkHolder.kt
nodes.summary: Small holder providing the Onyx refresher instance to reader composables.

nodes.name: GroupBrowseViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel exposing a sealed GroupBrowseUiState for one shelf, browsing its series via the MediaSource and falling back to downloaded works when offline.

nodes.name: GroupsViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel for the shelves overview: streams shelves and server config, loads cover collages and source containers, and persists group create/update/delete.

nodes.name: LibraryViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel for the main library: browses series, resolves effective content types per series, serves local works offline, and orchestrates per-series downloads through DownloadManager.

nodes.name: ComicReaderViewModel
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel driving guided comic reading: caches detected panels per page, navigates panel-by-panel via taps and hardware buttons through GuidedNavigator, and manages zoom and chrome state.

nodes.name: ComicPageLoader
nodes.summary: Decodes a comic page via Coil, downscales it for detection, and runs PanelDetector to return reading-ordered panel rectangles with page dimensions.

nodes.name: ReaderRoute.kt
nodes.summary: Reader host composable that observes ReaderViewModel content and dispatches to the Epub, Paged, Webtoon, or Comic reader screen based on content type and viewer mode, while toggling the Onyx e-ink fast-refresh mode on enter/exit.
nodes.languageNotes: Jetpack Compose router using a when-over-sealed-ReaderContent to keep viewer selection deterministic (Seam B).

nodes.name: ReaderRoute
nodes.summary: Composable host that collects reader state flows and renders the matching viewer screen per ReaderContent and ViewerMode, wiring hardware buttons, chrome toggle, and the Onyx refresher.

nodes.name: ReaderViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel driving the reader: loads a book from local download or stream, builds the cross-chapter webtoon strip, renders EPUB pages via MuPDF, handles hardware-button paging, and pushes read progress back to the source.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Kotlin coroutines/StateFlow + a Mutex-guarded MuPDF render cache; offline-first load path falls back from local download to network streaming.

nodes.name: ReaderViewModel
nodes.summary: Coordinates book loading (local-or-stream), webtoon-strip assembly, EPUB rendering through MuPDF, page navigation from hardware buttons, and server progress push.

nodes.name: ViewerMode.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of the runtime reader presentation modes (PAGED, WEBTOON, COMIC) selected by the reader host.

nodes.name: WebtoonReaderScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Continuous vertical-scroll webtoon viewer composable that lazily loads strip pages via Coil/FilteredReaderImage, supports hardware-button frame steps, tap-to-toggle chrome, and gated e-ink refresh.

nodes.name: WebtoonReaderScreen
nodes.summary: Lazy-column webtoon viewer rendering authenticated page images, reacting to frame-step events and reporting the settled page for progress tracking.

nodes.name: SeriesDetailScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Series detail screen showing the e-ink hero card, metadata, chapter list/grid, download controls, and the content-type/viewer override menu, rendered with the flat black-bordered Onyx design language.
nodes.languageNotes: Large Compose screen composed of many private composables; relies on EinkModal/EinkButtons/AnchoredMenuPopup and FilteredImage for the device-native look.

nodes.name: SeriesDetailViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel for the series detail screen: loads books and rich series metadata through the MediaSource seam, resolves per-book viewer types, manages serial/single downloads with progress and cancel, and applies optimistic read/type/progress overlays without full reloads.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines baseState with optimistic override StateFlows via combine; uses flatMapLatest over server config and WhileSubscribed sharing to avoid E-Ink loading flashes.

nodes.name: SeriesDetailViewModel
nodes.summary: Drives series detail state: book/metadata loading, viewer-type resolution, optimistic read/type/progress overlays, serial and per-chapter downloads with throttled progress, and dirty-progress sync.

nodes.name: ColorFilterSettingsContent.kt
nodes.summary: Color-filter settings UI: lists profiles, lets the user pick the active one, and opens a live editor with cover preview and sliders for saturation/contrast/brightness/levels/gamma/sharpen/dither tuned for E-Ink.

nodes.name: ColorFilterViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel for the color-filter editor: observes profiles and the active profile, loads random cover previews with auth headers, and edits/clamps profile parameters before persisting custom profiles.

nodes.name: ColorFilterViewModel
nodes.summary: Manages color-profile selection, in-memory cover preview with history, and a clamped live edit state that can be saved as a new or updated custom profile.

nodes.name: SettingsContent.kt
nodes.summary: Renders the body of a settings section (theme, language, display mode, server, webtoon overlap, guided overlay) with E-Ink components and search-term highlighting.

nodes.name: SettingsScreen.kt
nodes.summary: Top-level settings screen: a tile-landing of sections with search, navigating into per-section content in the Onyx-native flat layout.

nodes.name: SettingsSearch.kt
nodes.summary: Pure helper functions for settings search: computing case-insensitive match ranges for highlighting and testing whether a section matches a query.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Kotlin functions, deliberately side-effect free for unit testing.

nodes.name: matchRanges
nodes.summary: Returns all case-insensitive inclusive index ranges where a query occurs in a text, for highlighting search hits.

nodes.name: SettingsSections.kt
nodes.summary: Declarative registry of settings sections with their titles, icons, and searchable terms used by the settings screen tile landing and search.

nodes.name: SettingsViewModel.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt ViewModel exposing app settings flows (theme, language, display mode, download dir, webtoon overlap, guided overlay, server, active color profile) and the setters that persist them.

nodes.name: SettingsViewModel
nodes.summary: Bridges SettingsRepository, ServerRepository, and ColorProfileRepository into observable state flows and provides setters for persisting settings and saving/clearing the server connection.

nodes.name: EinkTokens.kt
nodes.summary: Central E-Ink design tokens (border widths, corner radii, spacing) shared across the flat Onyx-look UI.

nodes.name: Theme.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the monochrome high-contrast Material3 theme (light/dark inverted) with no accent color, enforcing the E-Ink design language.

nodes.name: network_security_config.xml
nodes.summary: Android network security configuration permitting cleartext/self-hosted HTTP traffic so users can connect to local or self-hosted Komga servers.

nodes.name: SeriesFilterTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the library series filtering logic over ContentType and Series domain models.

nodes.name: SettingsSearchTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the pure settings-search helpers, verifying match-range computation and section matching for set and empty queries.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Root Gradle build script declaring plugin versions/aliases shared across the multi-module Android project.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project guide documenting the non-negotiable architecture invariants (two seams), module boundaries, and detail-rule references for the Komga E-Ink reader.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build script for the data module, configuring Room, Hilt, and the persistence/download dependencies.

nodes.name: ColorProfileSeedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented test verifying that the AppDatabase seeds the built-in color-filter profiles on fresh install and self-heals an empty profile table.

nodes.name: PersistenceInstrumentedTest.kt
nodes.summary: Instrumented tests exercising the Room-backed server/settings repositories and Keystore credential store against a real AppDatabase.

nodes.name: AndroidManifest.xml
nodes.summary: Minimal Android manifest stub for the data library module.

nodes.name: AppDatabase.kt
nodes.summary: Room database definition with all entities/DAOs (version 11), the full set of versioned migrations 1→11, color-profile seeding, and an onCreate/onOpen callback that self-heals an empty profile table.
nodes.languageNotes: Migrations deliberately recreate tables instead of ALTER ADD COLUMN with NOT NULL defaults to avoid Room's destructive-migration data wipe.

nodes.name: AppDatabase
nodes.summary: Abstract Room database declaring all entities and exposing the DAO accessors for settings, server, downloads, shelves, overrides, read-progress, and color profiles.

nodes.name: ColorProfileDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the color_profiles table, providing observe/upsert/delete queries for E-Ink color filter profiles.
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin Room @Dao interface; queries return Flow for reactive observation.

nodes.name: DownloadDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the downloads table, exposing queries for locally stored offline books.

nodes.name: Entities.kt
nodes.summary: Defines all Room @Entity data classes (settings, server, shelves, series_overrides, read_progress, downloads, color_profiles); every cross-source record carries a sourceId.
nodes.languageNotes: Server credentials are stored as AES/GCM ciphertext + IV Base64 strings; plaintext never leaves the Keystore boundary.

nodes.name: ReadProgressDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the read_progress table backing the offline-first reading-progress sync queue.

nodes.name: SeriesOverrideDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the series_overrides table storing manual per-series content-type assignments across sources.

nodes.name: ServerDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the server table holding the configured backend connection and its encrypted credentials.

nodes.name: SettingsDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the key-value settings table providing reactive get/set access to app preferences.

nodes.name: ShelfDao.kt
nodes.summary: Room DAO for the shelves table backing user-defined libraries with encoded source lists.

nodes.name: DataModule.kt
nodes.summary: Hilt module wiring the Room AppDatabase (with migrations and seed callback) and providing all Room-backed repository and credential-store bindings as singletons.
nodes.languageNotes: Dagger/Hilt @Module @Provides; database builder registers migrations 1→11 plus a seed callback for built-in color profiles.

nodes.name: DownloadManager.kt
nodes.summary: Writes downloaded book bytes to a SAF tree URI (or internal storage fallback), records the download in the DB, and reads/deletes stored books across file-path and content-URI variants.
nodes.languageNotes: Coroutine IO dispatcher for blocking storage; supports both content:// SAF URIs and plain file paths.

nodes.name: RoomColorProfileRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed ColorProfileRepository managing E-Ink color profiles, with the active-profile pointer injected from the settings KV table to keep a single responsibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Entity<->domain mapping via private extension functions; built-in profiles are guarded against mutation.

nodes.name: RoomDownloadRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed DownloadRepository mapping download entities to/from the domain DownloadedBook model.

nodes.name: RoomReadProgressRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed ReadProgressRepository persisting offline-first per-book reading progress with dirty flags for the sync queue.

nodes.name: RoomSeriesOverrideRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed SeriesOverrideRepository persisting manual per-series content-type overrides keyed by sourceId and series remote id.

nodes.name: RoomServerRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed ServerRepository persisting the server connection and encrypting/decrypting its credentials via the Keystore-backed credential store.

nodes.name: RoomSettingsRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed SettingsRepository exposing typed reactive accessors over the key-value settings table.

nodes.name: RoomShelfRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Room-backed ShelfRepository managing user libraries, encoding/decoding shelf source lists via the ShelfSourceCodec.

nodes.name: CredentialStore.kt
nodes.summary: Interface abstracting secret storage (API key, password) so callers depend only on the contract, not the concrete encrypted implementation.

nodes.name: EncryptedCredentialStore.kt
nodes.summary: CredentialStore implementation backed by EncryptedSharedPreferences (AES256-GCM master key), with corrupt-keyset recovery and an in-memory-only fallback that never writes plaintext to disk.
nodes.languageNotes: Defensive recovery: deletes a corrupt prefs file and retries once, then falls back to RAM-only storage rather than crashing or leaking plaintext.

nodes.name: KeystoreCredentialStore.kt
nodes.summary: Encrypts/decrypts secrets with an AES/GCM key from the AndroidKeyStore, returning persistable ciphertext+IV blobs that survive app updates; the credential persistence itself is delegated to RoomServerRepository.
nodes.languageNotes: Keystore-anchored AES/GCM key survives adb install -r; CipherBlob holds Base64 ciphertext + IV for Room persistence.

nodes.name: RoomColorProfileRepositoryTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for RoomColorProfileRepository covering profile observation, active-pointer handling, and built-in mutation guards.

nodes.name: RoomSettingsRepositoryTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for RoomSettingsRepository verifying key-value read/write semantics over the settings DAO.

nodes.name: RoomShelfRepositoryEncodingTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the shelf source list encoding/decoding round-trip used by RoomShelfRepository.

nodes.name: viewer-type-resolution.md
nodes.summary: Documents the binding 6-stage domain rule for deterministic viewer-type resolution, the Komga reading-direction field mapping (Seam A), and the ViewerMode app mapping.

nodes.name: detail-redesign.html
nodes.summary: Static HTML mockup of the redesigned series-detail screen, used as a visual reference for the E-Ink UI layout.

nodes.name: DownloadManager
nodes.summary: Singleton that stores, reads, and deletes downloaded book files via SAF or internal storage and records each download in the repository.

nodes.name: KeystoreCredentialStore
nodes.summary: AES/GCM encrypt/decrypt service backed by the AndroidKeyStore, producing persistable CipherBlob values and implementing the CredentialStore interface.

nodes.name: EncryptedCredentialStore
nodes.summary: CredentialStore implementation over EncryptedSharedPreferences with corrupt-keyset recovery and a RAM-only fallback that never persists plaintext.

nodes.name: RoomColorProfileRepository
nodes.summary: Room-backed implementation of ColorProfileRepository with an injected active-profile pointer flow and setter.

nodes.name: RoomServerRepository
nodes.summary: Room-backed ServerRepository that persists the server connection and encrypts/decrypts credentials through the Keystore credential store.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-eink-color-filter.md
nodes.summary: 14-task implementation plan for the E-Ink color filter Phase 1: a pure ColorProfile/buildColorMatrix domain core, ColorProfileRepository with Room persistence (v6->v7 migration plus built-in seeds), and an app-layer LocalImageFilter with FilteredAsyncImage/FilteredImage wrappers, StepperRow control, ColorFilterViewModel and settings screen.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-guided-comic-reader.md

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-komga-libraries-viewer-detection.md
nodes.summary: 10-task plan adding real per-book viewer detection (readingDirection through Komga mapping, ResolveViewerType priority rule on seam B) and Komga libraries via a ContainerSource interface, Shelf persistence with v5->v6 migration, and library create/edit modal.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-2-mupdf-spike.md
nodes.summary: Short Phase 1 plan (2/4) documenting the verified MuPDF engine spike that confirmed the render engine choice, with conclusions and device-bound work deferred to plan 1.4.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-3-komga-source.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (3/4) for the :source-komga module: kotlinx.serialization DTOs, Retrofit API, mediaType->BookFormat detection, KomgaMapper, an X-API-Key auth interceptor, and KomgaSource with factory, all verified via MockWebServer integration and E2E tests.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4a-app-skeleton.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (4a) bootstrapping the :app module: Gradle/AGP setup, type-safe DE+EN i18n, E-Ink light/dark theme tokens, library and settings screens with navigation and MainActivity, verified by an emulator smoke test.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4b-render-core.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (4b) for the :render-core module wrapping MuPDF: DocumentFactory.open with a format hint, MupdfDocument and MupdfDocumentFactory, validated by an instrumented emulator E2E rendering all three formats.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4c-persistence.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 plan (4c) building the persistence foundation with Hilt and Room: domain repository interfaces, the :data module with entities/DAO/Database, repository implementations, a Hilt DI module, and an instrumented persistence E2E.

nodes.name: 2026-06-06-phase1-4d-library-ui.md

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build config for the pure-Kotlin domain module, deliberately free of Android, network, and source dependencies to enforce the architecture seams.

nodes.name: ColorFilterMatrix.kt
nodes.summary: Builds a row-major 4x5 ColorMatrix combining saturation (Rec.709 luminance), contrast, and brightness into a single GPU-compatible linear transform for covers and reader pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Analytically composes contrast/brightness scale+offset into one matrix to avoid chaining multiple ColorMatrix passes.

nodes.name: buildColorMatrix
nodes.summary: Computes the 20-element row-major color matrix for given saturation, contrast, and brightness in the 0..255 value range.

nodes.name: PixelPipeline.kt
nodes.summary: Pure-Kotlin Phase-2 pixel kernel processing ARGB arrays in-place through linear filter, levels, gamma, unsharp mask, and dithering, skipping neutral stages for E-Ink reader pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-channel bit manipulation on packed ARGB ints; Floyd-Steinberg and ordered Bayer dithering implemented from scratch.

nodes.name: buildGammaLut
nodes.summary: Builds a 256-entry gamma-correction lookup table; returns identity when gamma equals 1.

nodes.name: applyPixelPipeline
nodes.summary: Applies the full filter pipeline (linear, levels, gamma, unsharp, dithering) in-place to an ARGB pixel array, conditionally skipping neutral stages.

nodes.name: buildToneLut
nodes.summary: Builds a combined 256-entry lookup table for black/white-point levels stretching followed by gamma.

nodes.name: EinkController.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the device-side render seam (Seam B): the EinkController interface plus refresh-mode, region, button, and capability value types, abstracting Boox-specific E-Ink behavior from a No-Op fallback.

nodes.name: EinkController
nodes.summary: Interface encapsulating E-Ink device specifics: exposing capabilities and button events, and controlling region refresh, contrast, and inversion.

nodes.name: Book.kt
nodes.summary: Source-neutral domain data class for a book, carrying source/series identifiers, format, page count, download state, and server-reported read position metadata.

nodes.name: BookFormat.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of supported book file formats used by the source-neutral domain model.

nodes.name: ColorProfile.kt
nodes.summary: Source-/device-neutral E-Ink color filter profile holding linear and Phase-2 pixel parameters, with computed properties deciding whether the GPU matrix or full pixel pipeline is needed.

nodes.name: ColorProfile
nodes.summary: Data class for a named color filter profile, exposing isLinearNeutral/needsPixelPipeline/isNeutral derivations and an OFF fallback companion constant.

nodes.name: ContentType.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of content types used to deterministically drive viewer-type resolution.

nodes.name: DisplayMode.kt
nodes.summary: Enum distinguishing E-Ink versus standard display modes that gate animations and refresh behavior.

nodes.name: DitherMode.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of dithering modes (none, Floyd-Steinberg, ordered) consumed by the pixel pipeline.

nodes.name: DownloadState.kt
nodes.summary: Enum representing a book's offline download state for offline-first reading.

nodes.name: ReadingDirection.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of reading directions that feeds deterministic viewer-type resolution.

nodes.name: ReadProgress.kt
nodes.summary: Domain data class for a book's read progress with a dirty flag for offline-first sync and a locator for EPUB/comic positions.

nodes.name: Series.kt
nodes.summary: Source-agnostic domain data class for a series with generic metadata (summary, status, genres, reading direction) and a per-series content-type override for viewer resolution.

nodes.name: Shelf.kt
nodes.summary: Domain model for a shelf (app library) that groups series across sources, carrying an optional default content type used to drive deterministic viewer resolution.

nodes.name: SourceKind.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of supported backend source kinds (Komga, OPDS, local, unknown) used to tag each MediaSource without leaking concrete source types into the domain.

nodes.name: ViewerType.kt
nodes.summary: Enum of reader modes (PAGED, WEBTOON, COMIC, EPUB) that the viewer-type resolution rule maps onto, representing Seam B's render targets.

nodes.name: WebtoonStrip.kt
nodes.summary: Pure-Kotlin model that maps a global, cross-chapter page index to a (chapter, page-in-chapter) position and back, enabling chapter-accurate progress tracking during seamless webtoon scrolling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a prefix-sum IntArray of chapter starts; index lookups clamp out-of-range values and skip zero-page chapters.

nodes.name: Document.kt
nodes.summary: Seam B render abstraction: the Android-free Document/DocumentFactory interfaces plus the RenderedPage value type, letting MuPDF (or a later alternative engine) render all formats behind one contract without touching the reader or UI.
nodes.languageNotes: RenderedPage overrides equals/hashCode for content-based comparison of the IntArray pixel buffer.

nodes.name: ColorProfileRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository interface for persisting and observing E-Ink color profiles used by the central color filter.

nodes.name: DownloadRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository interface for managing offline downloads of books for source-agnostic offline-first reading.

nodes.name: KomgaUrl.kt
nodes.summary: Helper for constructing or normalizing Komga server base URLs used when configuring a server connection.

nodes.name: ReadProgressRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository interface for the offline-first read-progress store, exposing local progress with a dirty flag and a sync queue for server reconciliation.

nodes.name: SeriesOverrideRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository interface for per-series manual content-type overrides that take top priority in viewer-type resolution.

nodes.name: ServerRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository interface for persisting configured backend servers/source connections.

nodes.name: SettingsRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository interface for observing and persisting app settings such as display mode and E-Ink options.

nodes.name: ShelfRepository.kt
nodes.summary: Repository interface for persisting and observing shelves (app libraries) that group series across sources.

nodes.name: MediaSource.kt
nodes.summary: Seam A core: the MediaSource interface family (MediaSource, BrowsableSource, SyncingSource, ContainerSource) plus value types (PagedResult, SourceFilter, PageRef, SourceContainer) that every backend implements behind a source-agnostic contract.
nodes.languageNotes: Capability-based interface segregation: optional source abilities (browse, sync, container listing) are separate interfaces that concrete sources implement only when supported.

nodes.name: SourceId.kt
nodes.summary: Generates globally unique, deterministic 64-bit source IDs from name/kind/config via SHA-256 (Mihon-style), with ID 0 reserved for the local source, so multiple servers coexist without a central registry.
nodes.languageNotes: Hashes a normalized key with MessageDigest SHA-256 and folds the first 8 bytes into a Long, clearing the sign bit for a non-negative ID.

nodes.name: SourceManager.kt
nodes.summary: Reactive registry of active sources (id to MediaSource) exposed as a StateFlow; getOrStub guarantees a missing backend never breaks the library by substituting a StubSource.
nodes.languageNotes: Backed by a MutableStateFlow updated immutably with map +/- operators; no CoroutineScope kept in the domain module.

nodes.name: StubSource.kt
nodes.summary: Placeholder MediaSource that holds only id/name for library entries whose real source is unavailable, keeping the library intact while browsing and sync intentionally fail.

nodes.name: ResolveProgressConflict.kt
nodes.summary: Offline-first sync conflict use case: the newest read-progress (highest updatedAt) wins, with local winning on ties or when the remote is absent.

nodes.name: ResolveShelfContentType.kt
nodes.summary: Use case that derives a series' shelf default content type path-independently via container/library membership, so the shelf tag applies even when the series is opened through browse/search.

nodes.name: ResolveViewerType.kt
nodes.summary: Deterministic per-book reader-mode resolver (Seam B) applying a fixed six-step priority rule from series override down to format default, never relying on fragile content auto-detection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an invoke operator for the per-book rule plus forContentType for shelf-level resolution; the library default deliberately precedes the format default so a WEBTOON tag beats CBZ's PAGED default.

nodes.summary: Capability interface for sources that can be browsed and read: browse, search, list books and pages, fetch rich series detail, and open raw page bytes.

nodes.name: ContainerSource
nodes.summary: Optional capability interface for sources that can list their top-level containers (Komga libraries, OPDS feeds).

nodes.name: Document
nodes.summary: Seam B render interface providing page count, page size, and per-page rendering with zoom and rotation, abstracting the underlying engine (MuPDF or alternative).

nodes.name: SourceManager
nodes.summary: Reactive source registry exposing active sources as a StateFlow with register/unregister/get and a getOrStub fallback that keeps the library functional when a backend is missing.

nodes.name: StubSource
nodes.summary: Null-object MediaSource holding only id and name for unavailable backends, so the library never breaks; kind is UNKNOWN.

nodes.name: ResolveViewerType
nodes.summary: Use case implementing the deterministic six-step viewer-type priority rule, plus a forContentType shortcut for shelf-level resolution and a private ContentType-to-ViewerType map.

nodes.name: ResolveShelfContentType
nodes.summary: Use case resolving a series' shelf default content type via container/library membership, independent of how the series was opened.

nodes.name: ResolveProgressConflict
nodes.summary: Use case applying the offline-first conflict rule: newest updatedAt wins, local wins on tie or when remote is null.

nodes.name: WebtoonStrip
nodes.summary: Pure model mapping a cross-chapter global page index to a (chapter, page-in-chapter) position and back via prefix sums, with clamping and zero-page chapter skipping.

nodes.name: of
nodes.summary: Builds a deterministic non-negative 64-bit source ID by SHA-256 hashing a normalized name/kind/config key and folding the first 8 bytes into a Long.

nodes.name: BorderLineSplit.kt
nodes.summary: Recursively splits a region along narrow dark axis-aligned border lines bounded by lighter bands, separating black-framed/touching panels without fragmenting solid ink areas.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Kotlin operating on a BooleanArray dark mask with neighbor-context heuristics; host-testable, no Android dependency.

nodes.name: GuidedNavigator.kt
nodes.summary: Stateless navigator stepping forward/backward through the ordered panel list for the guided comic reading flow.

nodes.name: GutterFill.kt
nodes.summary: Flood-fills the background gutter/margin network reachable from the page edges using 4-connectivity, seeding only light edge pixels.

nodes.name: ImageBinarization.kt
nodes.summary: Binarizes a rendered page via Rec.601 luminance and an adaptive Otsu threshold to produce a background mask for panel detection.

nodes.name: Panel.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the PanelRect data type holding integer pixel-space panel bounds.

nodes.name: PanelDetector.kt
nodes.summary: Orchestrates hybrid panel detection: Otsu binarization, edge gutter flood-fill, connected-component labeling, border-line splitting, blob/containment filtering, then reading-order sort.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-Kotlin XY-cut successor: flood-fill from edges separates panels even with art bleeding into gutters.

nodes.name: PanelGeometry.kt
nodes.summary: Pure geometry helpers for the comic reader: normalize panel coordinates, hit-test taps, and compute crop-free fit-to-viewport zoom scale.

nodes.name: ReadingOrder.kt
nodes.summary: Sorts panel boxes into reading order by grouping into vertically-overlapping row bands then ordering left-to-right or right-to-left per reading direction.

nodes.name: RegionLabeling.kt
nodes.summary: Labels connected non-flooded regions (gutter-enclosed panels) via 8-connectivity BFS and returns their bounding boxes.

nodes.name: OnyxEinkController
nodes.summary: EinkController implementation for Onyx Boox hardware, exposing reader-lifecycle hooks (enter/exit fast mode, full refresh, per-view update mode) and the EinkController interface methods.

nodes.name: OnyxRefresher
nodes.summary: Hilt singleton wrapping OnyxEinkController with reader-facing helpers for fast-mode toggling and interval-based ghost-clearing full refreshes.

nodes.name: BorderLineSplit
nodes.summary: Object that recursively splits panel regions at dark border-line bands, picking the strongest horizontal or vertical cut while rejecting uniform dark areas via neighbor-context checks.

nodes.name: PanelDetector
nodes.summary: Detects comic panels from a RenderedPage through the full binarize-flood-label-split-filter-sort pipeline, with configurable minimum area and containment thresholds.

nodes.name: PanelGeometry
nodes.summary: Object providing pure geometry operations: normalize panel rects, hit-test normalized taps, max area fraction, and contain-fit zoom scale.

nodes.name: ImageBinarization
nodes.summary: Object computing luminance, the Otsu threshold, and the boolean background mask for a rendered page.

nodes.name: GutterFill
nodes.summary: Object performing iterative 4-connectivity flood fill of the edge-reachable background gutter network.

nodes.name: RegionLabeling
nodes.summary: Object labeling connected non-flooded regions via 8-connectivity BFS, returning panel bounding boxes.

nodes.name: ReadingOrder
nodes.summary: Object sorting panels into reading order by row banding and LTR/RTL ordering within each band.

layers.name: Presentation & App Shell
layers.description: Jetpack Compose UI, ViewModels, the multi-reader viewer host (paged comic, webtoon, EPUB-reflow), navigation, theme, i18n and Hilt DI wiring — the top imperative shell that wires every module together.

layers.name: Domain Core
layers.description: Pure-Kotlin domain models, use cases, repository interfaces and the two architectural seam contracts (MediaSource for sources, Document/EinkController for render+e-ink) with no Android, network or source dependencies.

layers.name: Media Sources (Seam A)
layers.description: Concrete MediaSource implementations behind Seam A — Komga REST and OPDS — each depending only on domain, with their own Retrofit/serialization DTOs and mappers, sharing no cross-source knowledge.

layers.name: Render & E-Ink Engine (Seam B)
layers.description: Render and device seam behind Seam B — MuPDF-backed Document/PageRenderer (render-core), Onyx Boox e-ink controller (eink-onyx), and pure-Kotlin XY-cut guided-view panel detection — all UI-independent.

layers.name: Data & Persistence
layers.description: Room database, DAOs, repository implementations, sync/download manager and encrypted credential storage, with every record carrying a sourceId for the source-agnostic library.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Design specs, phased implementation plans, the project guide and the detail-redesign HTML mockup that drive the spec-to-plan-to-build workflow.

layers.name: Build & Tooling
layers.description: Gradle Kotlin-DSL build scripts, version catalog and wrapper, Android manifests, the Node icon generator, the e2e smoke script and the throwaway MuPDF/Komga spikes.

tour.title: The Architecture Spec
tour.description: Start here: this is the foundational design spec for the whole app. It frames the problem and vision (a source- and device-agnostic e-ink reader for Onyx Boox), then introduces the single most important idea you must carry through the rest of the tour: TWO SEAMS that encapsulate all variability. Seam A abstracts media sources (Komga, OPDS), Seam B abstracts render and e-ink hardware. It also lays out the Gradle module cut, the domain model, and the AGPL licensing constraint.

tour.title: The Project Guide & Invariants
tour.description: CLAUDE.md is the condensed contract that turns the spec into non-negotiable rules. It restates the two seams, the per-module dependency boundaries (domain depends on nothing Android/network/source-specific), and the deterministic viewer-type resolution rule. Read it as the map that tells you which modules may import which — the rest of the tour follows these boundaries top-down.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: MainActivity is the runtime entry point and the root of the BFS dependency traversal. As the single Android Activity it hosts the entire Compose navigation graph (home, series, group, reader routes), wires hardware volume keys to page turns, and binds theme, language, e-ink mode and the active color filter. KomgaReaderApp is the Hilt application that boots dependency injection and detects whether the device is real Onyx hardware. Together they are the imperative shell that wires every module behind the seams.
tour.languageLesson: Compose composition locals (LocalEinkMode, LocalStrings, LocalImageFilter) thread cross-cutting state — e-ink mode, language, color filter — down the UI tree without prop-drilling, the Compose analogue of React context.

tour.title: Browsing the Library
tour.description: Building on the entry point's navigation graph, HomeScreen is the top-level scaffold with the bottom navigation between library, groups, plugins and settings. LibraryViewModel is the busiest UI brain in the app: it browses series through the active MediaSource (Seam A), resolves each series' effective content type, and falls back to locally downloaded works when offline. Notice it never names Komga or OPDS directly — it only knows the abstract source, which is the whole point of Seam A.

tour.title: Series Detail & Viewer-Type Resolution
tour.description: Tapping a series opens SeriesDetailViewModel, which loads rich metadata through the MediaSource seam and — critically — resolves which reading mode each book should open in. ResolveViewerType encodes the deterministic 6-stage priority rule (per-series override → shelf content type → reading direction → format), and the companion doc spells the rule out verbatim. This is the hinge between browsing (Seam A) and reading (Seam B): the resolved ViewerType decides which reader screen renders next.

tour.title: The Reader Host & Three Modes
tour.description: ReaderRoute is the dispatcher: it observes the loaded book and the resolved viewer mode (PAGED comic, WEBTOON scroll, EPUB reflow) and hands off to the matching reader screen, toggling Onyx fast-refresh on enter and exit. ReaderViewModel is the shared engine behind all modes — it loads a book from local download or stream, builds the cross-chapter webtoon strip, renders EPUB pages via MuPDF, routes hardware-button paging, and pushes read progress back to the source. This is the shared structure that all viewers build on.

tour.title: Seam B: Render & E-Ink Contracts
tour.description: Now descend below the reader into Seam B itself. Document is the render contract: a backend (MuPDF) turns any cbz/cbr/pdf/epub into an Android Bitmap, decoupling the render target from the view. EinkController is the device contract that the readers in Step 6 drive through — its OnyxRefresher implementation schedules partial-versus-full refreshes to fight ghosting on real Boox hardware, while a no-op fallback keeps development on an emulator from ever crashing. Everything above these interfaces is device-agnostic.

tour.title: Seam A: The MediaSource Interface
tour.description: Seam A is the other load-bearing abstraction. MediaSource (with its BrowsableSource and SyncingSource refinements) is the only thing the UI and domain ever know about a backend — it is the most depended-upon source contract in the graph. KomgaSourceProvider is the small factory that constructs a concrete source from persisted server config, bridging the app shell to the seam without leaking source-specific types upward.
tour.languageLesson: Kotlin splits one capability into several small interfaces (MediaSource / BrowsableSource / SyncingSource) so a source only implements what it can do — interface segregation that keeps a future OPDS or plugin source from being forced to fake progress sync.

tour.title: Concrete Sources Behind Seam A
tour.description: These are the implementations that live behind the Step 8 interface. KomgaSource speaks the Komga REST API via Retrofit; KomgaMapper translates its serialization DTOs into the neutral domain models, applying the .ifBlank-to-null pattern so optional metadata stays clean. OpdsSource proves the seam holds: a completely different protocol implements the same contract and shares zero Komga knowledge. Each source module depends only on domain — never on the app, never on each other.

tour.title: The Domain Core
tour.description: Everything above the seams ultimately speaks this vocabulary. Series, ContentType and Book are the highest-fan-in types in the whole codebase — the shared nouns flowing through every layer. ServerRepository is the most depended-upon contract overall. This pure-Kotlin module has no Android, network or source dependency (the build script in Step 12 enforces that), so its models and use cases are trivially unit-testable. This is the stable center the two seams plug into.

tour.title: Data, Persistence & Offline-First
tour.description: The data module implements the domain repository interfaces from Step 10 with Room. AppDatabase (schema version 11, with versioned migrations and a self-healing seed callback) and Entities define the on-device schema — every cross-source record carries a sourceId so the library stays source-agnostic. RoomReadProgressRepository persists reading progress with dirty flags, which is the heart of the offline-first sync queue: read offline now, push to the source later. DataModule is the Hilt wiring that binds it all as singletons.
tour.languageLesson: Room migrations must be incremental and ordered (1→2→...→11); each schema change ships a migration so an existing install upgrades without a destructive wipe. A naive ALTER ADD COLUMN with a default can silently trigger a full table recreate — hence the explicit migrations plus the onOpen self-heal.

tour.title: The E-Ink Color Pipeline
tour.description: A standalone seam worth its own stop: the e-ink color filter. ColorProfile and the pure ColorFilterMatrix domain core describe saturation/contrast/gamma adjustments tuned for the Boox Kaleido display, with no Android dependency. ColorPipelineTransformation applies that profile to decoded reader-page bitmaps as a cached Coil transformation. Like the main seams, the policy (which adjustments) is pure and testable while the pixel work happens at the imperative edge.

tour.title: Build, DI Wiring & Licensing
tour.description: The capstone ties the architecture back to its enforcement. The domain build script is deliberately free of Android, network and source dependencies — the seam boundaries are enforced by Gradle, not by convention. libs.versions.toml is the shared version catalog across all modules. AppModule is the Hilt module that performs the Seam B device selection (real OnyxEinkController on Boox, no-op otherwise). NOTICE records the AGPL-3.0-or-later obligation that MuPDF imposes on the whole app, plus the Lucide icon attribution.
